task.en:
Using TextEdit, create a new document. If it opens in Rich Text mode, switch it to plain text first (Format menu → Make Plain Text, or ⇧⌘T). Type exactly the three lines below (no extra lines, no trailing blank line beyond a single newline at the end):
```
one
two
three
```
Save the file to `~/Desktop/notes.txt` (use ⌘S, name it `notes.txt`, choose the Desktop, and make sure the format is Plain Text).

pre_command: rm -f "$HOME/Desktop/notes.txt"; osascript -e 'tell application "TextEdit" to quit' 2>/dev/null; sleep 1
grading_command: awk 'NR==1{a=($0=="one")} NR==2{b=($0=="two")} NR==3{c=($0=="three")} END{ if(a&&b&&c) print "True"; else print "False" }' "$HOME/Desktop/notes.txt" 2>/dev/null

printf 'one
two
three
' > /tmp/_expected_notes.txt && cmp -s /tmp/_expected_notes.txt "$HOME/Desktop/notes.txt" && echo True || echo False
